title: Ranking Keys, Stable Sorts, Ties, and Top-K Reports · Runtime Lens: Ranking Reproducibility
summary: Equal-frequency words appear in hash-dependent order, reverse sorting reverses both count and token unexpectedly, top-k truncation happens before ranking, and repeated runs produce noisy diffs. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ranking reproducibility increment in deterministic ranked-frequency report. Lea…
objectives: Compare repeated and partitioned runs byte-for-byte and explain any locale, Unicode, or policy dependency.
Validate k, rank the complete eligible set or use a justified selection algorithm, and specify ties at the cutoff.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-ranking-determinism-2
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-ranking-determinism-2.
# Case 7417: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def worked_bookbot_ranking_determinism_2(eligible_words: int = 9, ranked_words: int = 9, top_k: int = 5, ties_resolved: bool = True, repeated_output_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "corpusworked2",
        "policy2dekrow",
        "revision7417",
        "commandworked27417",
        "report2dekrow7417",
        "test7417worked2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if eligible_words < 0 or ranked_words < 0:
        return False, ranked_words, "rank-shape"
    if ranked_words != eligible_words:
        return False, ranked_words, "rank-coverage"
    if top_k < 1 or top_k > ranked_words:
        return False, top_k, "top-k"
    if not ties_resolved or not repeated_output_equal:
        return False, top_k, "unstable-ranking"
    assert top_k <= ranked_words
    return True, top_k, "deterministic-top-k"

# Evidence: bookbot-ranking-reproducibility-1abct1
# Competency: bookbot-ranking-reproducibility.
# Case 6987: a clean-environment package release.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.

def evidence_bookbot_ranking_reproducibility_1abct1(eligible_words: int = 9, ranked_words: int = 9, top_k: int = 5, ties_resolved: bool = True, repeated_output_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1abct1 = "1abct1-1tcba1-6987"
    assert evidence_variant_1abct1
    evidence_axes_1abct1 = (
        "corpus1abct1",
        "policy1tcba1",
        "revision6987",
        "command1abct16987",
        "report1tcba16987",
        "test69871abct1",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1abct1)) == 6
    if eligible_words < 0 or ranked_words < 0:
        return False, ranked_words, "rank-shape"
    if ranked_words != eligible_words:
        return False, ranked_words, "rank-coverage"
    if top_k < 1 or top_k > ranked_words:
        return False, top_k, "top-k"
    if not ties_resolved or not repeated_output_equal:
        return False, top_k, "unstable-ranking"
    assert top_k <= ranked_words
    return True, top_k, "deterministic-top-k"

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Bookbot and Python Defend the Ranking Reproducibility model · Theory Ranking Reproducibility, keep Ranking Reproducibility connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare repeated and partitioned runs byte-for-byte and explain any locale, Unicode, or policy dependency.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Ranking Reproducibility model · Theory Ranking Reproducibility, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Ranking Reproducibility precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Ranking Reproducibility model · Theory Ranking Reproducibility, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 9480 during a digital-humanities corpus revision. Build deterministic Python evidence to rank all eligible words with explicit ties and reproducible top-k output; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare repeated and partitioned runs byte-for-byte and explain any locale, Unicode, or policy dependency.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Ranking Reproducibility model · Theory Ranking Reproducibility, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Deterministic output matters only to automated tests, not users.
